title: Did 111 Really Leave Out Anything Important
display_title: Did 111 Really Leave Out Anything Important
wikitext:
[]: Did 1.11 Really Leave Out Anything Important?             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Thu May 12, 2011 1:51 pm One of the criticisms you hear about YA(N)A is that it's too rushed, too edited. Yet I'm struggling to come up with many cuts that really hurt the film. Most scenes are reproduced in their entirety (or nearly so). Certainly episode four is the biggest loser... I know that one has its defenders (and I like parts of it too) but cutting it down to five minutes I think was best. Outside of that, I can't think of anything substantial that was deleted at all. In fact, risking blasphemy, I'm beginning to consider 1.11 to be a better representation of that part of the story than the original episodes. For the most part, the new film has the exact same animation, only better. Everything I loved from the arc is present, with some very cool tweaks (love the new training simulator). To top it off, they remake one of the series' lamer Angel battles into a sequence of staggering beauty (not even Rebuild's greatest detractor would deny that, I think). I have some criticisms (I do wish there was a tad more of Shinji at school) but they're nitpicks. Overall, 1.11 is a better encapsulation of those episodes than we could have hoped. But maybe I'm wrong. What did 1.11 leave out that was a deal breaker for you?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu May 12, 2011 2:02 pm No, 1.11 didn't really lack anything. Few scenes early on go by a bit too fast though. That's it.

[]:             Quote Post by Fallingtogrey » Thu May 12, 2011 2:12 pm I like it.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Thu May 12, 2011 2:15 pm Gonna have to watch it again to give you specifics, but as I recall I found it lacked the careful sense of pacing the show had, in addition to cutting out a lot of the exposition and, most importantly, character development. It played like a highlights reel of the most memorable scenes from the first six episodes, but the less memorable scenes in those episodes were still important. One minor quibble I have with it is that the clever thing the first two episodes did with the Unit 01/Sachiel fight - cutting straight to the aftermath and revealing the rest of it as a flashback - was completely scrapped, although that particular trick wouldn't have worked as well in a cinematic format, so I'm willing to forgive it. Other stuff in the movie, though, I wasn't so fond of. Anyway, I'm planning on marathoning both Rebuild films with some friends who haven't yet seen them next weekend, so maybe after that I'll be able to come back with some more specific statements. Incidentally I'm also planning to write a good-'n-proper review for 2.22 after seeing it again, so anyone who cares what I have to say, stay tuned. All none of you.

[]: Re: Did 1.11 Really Leave Out Anything Important?             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Thu May 12, 2011 2:23 pm Warren Peace wrote: One of the criticisms you hear about YA(N)A is that it's too rushed, too edited. Yet I'm struggling to come up with many cuts that really hurt the film.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Thu May 12, 2011 2:30 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu May 12, 2011 2:35 pm When I watched Eva with my sister (after having shown 1.X to her first), she got bored with the first few eps quickly, sice she hadn't missed anything THAT substantial, so we switched to ep 7 relatively quickly. (It makes one aware of the overall quality of EVA when your sister asks you why the hell episode 7 was left out in 2.X since it's so good. She hadn't seen the rest (including 2.X, I just told her about it - its better to see it AFTER the old series, that makes it even more awesome than it already is)yet, of course XD ) She just happened to be in the room when I watched 1.X, fell in luv with it, and promptly, both my sister demanded to see all of EVA, so the "drawing in new fans" bit seems to have worked... on them, at least. I wish I could bring my brother to watch it, but he wouldn't even agree to see 1. X XD In my opinion, the hand protecting Shinji scene was "too early" anyway, and the eye... cool, but not that substantial, time constraints and stuff. The "human hands" from the shamshel battle already provide enough "LOL WTF its organic". While there were scenes I missed (Misato comparing Shinji to his father, Kensuke in his tent, the angel DNA/ "where do the burns come from" scene, "Welcome home"... ) I understand why those were left out and others left or put in. I wouldn't say it's "better", it's just that it's a movie , that sort of works differently...

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu May 12, 2011 2:42 pm The Kensuke in his tent scene is important if and only if we are expected to pick up that all the kids in that class have lost their mothers. So far in the movies, we have no idea if that it still significant.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu May 12, 2011 2:44 pm Fallingtogrey wrote: Eva 01 moving to protect Shinji from falling rubble just before he pilots for the first time is missing. As is Shinji seeing Eva 01's eye. These were probably left out for a reason though.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Thu May 12, 2011 2:45 pm 1.0 is what really screwed up Rebuild if you ask me sure, I consider 2.0 a far better movie, but all of the flaws of that and the upcoming films sorely rest on what went down in the overall creation process of 1.0 1.0 should've been a giant declaration that "This is not your older brother's Evangelion", but instead...it repeated, I repeat...way too fuckin' much of the original series In my eyes, that's not a good way to start off a reboot. It's borderline Gus Van Sant's Psycho and John Moore's The Omen bullshit where it relies too much on the originals and replicates too much of said original work Sure, people would've complained if 1.0 was nearly nothing like the original series, but that would've ultimately been for the better for the series of films in the end run

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Thu May 12, 2011 2:47 pm Mr. Tines wrote: The Kensuke in his tent scene is important if and only if we are expected to pick up that all the kids in that class have lost their mothers. So far in the movies, we have no idea if that it still significant.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu May 12, 2011 2:47 pm Mr. Tines wrote: The Kensuke in his tent scene is important if and only if we are expected to pick up that all the kids in that class have lost their mothers. So far in the movies, we have no idea if that it still significant.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu May 12, 2011 2:49 pm Missing much of episode 4 which, for all its lack of Anno, was fantastic and the episode which convinced me to keep watching the show the first time I saw it. Obviously as nobody is even pretending Kensuke is relevant any longer, it makes perfect sense to cut his part out. But the fact that Shinji eventually decides when Section 2 will be returning him to Nerv and not the other way around does represent a very interesting difference between the original Shinji and Rebuild's Shinji, the second hint that his character has its very real differences from the kid in NGE. also why would you remove the movie theater scene Anno, why would you do that As has been pointed out already, a few details change to show that Gendou's plot in Rebuild revolves around getting Shinji to fuck Rei rather than around getting Yui's maternal instincts fired up. The fact that the thing where Shinji doesn't remember the fight until the night afterwards was cut irritates me a little - it was a brilliant storytelling device, although it worked much better in a TV episode's pacing than it would have in a film. It's actually left quite vague what Shinji does know about the Sachiel fight. There's also the other change where Shinji doesn't see Unit 01's face. What's the meaning of this? I can't imagine Shinji is supposed to be ignorant of the fact that the Evas are alive or something. It's assumed that everyone else at Nerv sees it, but Shinji appears to have blacked out the moment Sachiel shoots him through the head. It always interested me that the Committee is left out and we're just talking to SEELE from the very beginning - with such things as the Vatican Treaty, you'd think the regulatory body in control of the Evas would be less shady, not more so. Also, there's only seven of them? Where'd the other five go? I seem to recall that SEELE 12 actually said quite a lot in NGE (maybe not 12 but it was one of the higher-numbered ones). And while I'm at it, what's the logo on the back of the SEELE monoliths? It looks like the tripartite Magi symbol thing. Anyway, not a huge amount of stuff is cut from 1.11 because there wasn't a huge amount of stuff to cut away. The first six episodes of Eva could comfortably be arranged into a feature film on their own with no cuts at all and a relatively restrained runtime (albeit shitty pacing). Looking at what 2.22 is, hell, maybe more should have been cut and more new ground broken - it would have gone against the jo-ha-kyu thing that Anno is so hellbent on following, I guess, but I find it difficult to care about that pattern, to be honest, especially since it appears to be responsible for the majority of the "when it's done" nonsense that is proving such a detriment to the whole mess.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu May 12, 2011 3:00 pm From what the intierviews say, it's not a plan revolving around S/R, but one that merely involves it - or that the plan involves stuff happening like it did in the series and that Rei was just the next thing on the shedule/they mentioned, but that they thought that it be a nice idea to continue that when ppl got the impression that its a S/R plan. à la "Sure, why not? actually a nice idea!" I'm not sure which of those it was.../ its not that easy to understand what they mean.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Thu May 12, 2011 3:01 pm Azathoth wrote: also why would you remove the movie theater scene Anno, why would you do that

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu May 12, 2011 3:03 pm It#s not that much of a big, important iconic scene/ it doesn't tell us anything that we can't infer from watching the rest of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Thu May 12, 2011 3:09 pm I actually wouldn't have been surprised if Anno cut out episode four completely, being that he had nothing to do with it. As it stands, he just mostly cut it out (and, as Azathoth pointed out, changes an important part of it).

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu May 12, 2011 3:34 pm Kendrix wrote: From what the intierviews say, it's not a plan revolving around S/R

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu May 12, 2011 4:06 pm Actually, I can't see how Gendo's expression after Rei falls to the ground is significantly different in Rebuild - i always thought it was an interesting expression, some weirdly twisted mix between "It's gonna work!" (because he could tell from Shinji's reaction that he would most likely pilot) and "Oh noes, my little girl!" It's not quite the smirk, there's something about the edges of his mouth... There's no debating that the plan he seems to be following in 2.X involves Shinji triggering god mode. But whatever he's planning seems to play out itself in both films, with his only intervention being POSSIBLY telling Ritsuko to give the card to Shinji by means of Misato so he'd deliver it, and the stuff with Rei if he was actually trying to guilt-trip Shinji. While it is possible/a very valid theory/nothing Gendo wouldn't do, I think he's just being pragmatic here - The kid won't get into the EVA? Well, no use but to use Rei or poof end of the world. He went as far as calling Shinji, but he can't do more... Note that he dodges the question when Fuyutsuki proposes brainwashing/ says he'd rather use Rei. Also, he initially declines Rei's request to come to her party - it would be the ideal way to get the two together/ and he'd perfectly able to manipulate Shinji if he'd just adress a few nice-sounding lies at the kid, given how starved he is for Gendo's attention. But he prefers to keep himself away from Shinji, which is, if only in Gendo's own, biased world view, "good" for Shinji. It takes Rei's puppy stare + a glass of wine = Yui's puppy stare for him to give in... I think Yui used to prod him to take care of Shinji when they we're together/told him to have more confidence when he was afraid to do stuff wrong... He almost let their most effective pilot/one of his most useful tools walk out of the door twice, while he could have him kidnapped by MIB and brainwashed/ could have easily manipulated Shinji with some sweet words.... He WOULD do the nastiest things to Shinji if he has to (He frquently justifies his actions with "It's the only way to live" - well, the only way in Gendo's own, very negative worldview that he frequently displays in his little philosophic chats with Fuyu. His inability to comunicate with Shinji might've been one of the things that convinced him that Instrumentality is the only way - he seemed to have been with Yui/her plan to stop Seele before her death/leaving Shinji - and think of these little vague mentions of "It was YOU who ran away" during Shinji's head trips... Honestly, there is a LOT of vagueness there - what if baby Shinji, not really understanding stuff, blamed Gendo for his mother's death (he may have overheard ppl talking or saw his dad push buttons and then, suddenly, mommy was gone...) and flat out rejected him/ tried to run away from him, possibly accompained by lines like "You're not my Daddy anymore" or "bring back mommy right now!"? You know how Shinji is when he gets into "Do-not-want-mode"... Now, a normal person would be reasonable just try to explain stuff in an easy way/wait for the kid to calm down, but Gendo was a) Himself and b) REALLY overcome with grief(he suddenly gains a LOT of lines under his eyes when we see him again after Yui's death, he burned everything/ presumedly spent a week lying around staring at the ceiling in disbelief...), and ended up sending Shinji away... As for NGE!Gendo, I think he wanted to keep Shinji away from everything (He seemed to have wished for his kid to have a normal life in ep 20...) but then, Rei got injured, and the foretold arrival date of the angels came closer... he needed someone else to pilot unit 01, and he knew someone the mech would accept for sure - Yui's own son. Being the pragmatist he is, he called Shinji here and carried out his plan without changing it even if the one filling the spot of the "third child" was his own son. Yui, on the other hand, seemed to have wanted her kid to fill the role of the chosen one, hence bringing him along to see his future mech. I don't think she told Gendo... Rebuild!Gendo, however, seems to be in on the plan/seems to have expected to use Shinji as the third child. Seele did not... Maybe that's part of some little plan him, Fuyu and Yui had - there's a mention of a plan that was "forged 14 years ago" which implies Yui was still alive/involved in it. She probably did not know yet who would fill the spot of the "first child"... but that flashback where she tells Gendo to "take care of Shinji"... are these some sort of "parting words"? Hm. We'll see, we'll see... The "plan details" weren't told to us until late in the series, so I'd expect to hear of them in Q / early final. I say halfway through Q is the likeliest, tough. Or they insert the flashbacks while Shinji and Rei are stuck in Shogoki? Again, who knows. But there will be a "meeting of children whose fates have been predetermined...

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Thu May 12, 2011 4:19 pm I don't think 1.11 was missing anything. It's a pretty good movie. And as it stands, IMO, 2.22 was a masterpiece. Also, some people seem to be forgetting that little tidbit in the 2.22 pamphlet. It said Eva2.22 pamphlet wrote: The overall concept of the New Theatrical Edition could compared to that of a railroad. In Prelude, two trains depart from the same starting point, but sooner or later, they come across rail switch points and the familiar scenery starts to change. This is why the introductory part, which tries to start from the same starting point, is called a Prelude.

His inability to comunicate with Shinji might've been one of the things that convinced him that Instrumentality is the only way - he seemed to have been with Yui/her plan to stop Seele before her death/leaving Shinji - and think of these little

Article: Did 111 Really Leave Out Anything Important
ate with Shinji might've been one of the things that convinced him that Instrumentality is the only way - he seemed to have been with Yui/her plan to stop Seele before her death/leaving Shinji - and think of these little vague mentions of "It was YOU who ran away" during Shinji's head trips... Honestly, there is a LOT of vagueness there - what if baby Shinji, not really understanding stuff, blamed Gendo for his mother's death (he may have overheard ppl talking or saw his dad push buttons and then, suddenly, mommy was gone...) and flat out rejected him/ tried to run away from him, possibly accompained by lines like "You're not my Daddy anymore" or "bring back mommy right now!"? You know how Shinji is when he gets into "Do-not-want-mode"... Now, a normal person would be reasonable just try to explain stuff in an easy way/wait for the kid to calm down, but Gendo was a) Himself and b) REALLY overcome with grief(he suddenly gains a LOT of lines under his eyes when we see him again after Yui's death, he burned everything/ presumedly spent a week lying around staring at the ceiling in disbelief...), and ended up sending Shinji away... As for NGE!Gendo, I think he wanted to keep Shinji away from everything (He seemed to have wished for his kid to have a normal life in ep 20...) but then, Rei got injured, and the foretold arrival date of the angels came closer... he needed someone else to pilot unit 01, and he knew someone the mech would accept for sure - Yui's own son. Being the pragmatist he is, he called Shinji here and carried out his plan without changing it even if the one filling the spot of the "third child" was his own son. Yui, on the other hand, seemed to have wanted her kid to fill the role of the chosen one, hence bringing him along to see his future mech. I don't think she told Gendo... Rebuild!Gendo, however, seems to be in on the plan/seems to have expected to use Shinji as the third child. Seele did not... Maybe that's part of some little plan him, Fuyu and Yui had - there's a mention of a plan that was "forged 14 years ago" which implies Yui was still alive/involved in it. She

... Maybe that's part of some little plan him, Fuyu and Yui had - there's a mention of a plan that was "forged 14 years ago" which implies Yui was still alive/involved in it. She probably did not know yet who would fill the spot of the "first child"... but that flashback where she tells Gendo to "take care of Shinji"... are these some sort of "parting words"? Hm. We'll see, we'll see... The "plan details" weren't told to us until late in the series, so I'd expect to hear of them in Q / early final. I say halfway through Q is the likeliest, tough. Or they insert the flashbacks while Shinji and Rei are stuck in Shogoki? Again, who knows. But there will be a "meeting of children whose fates have been predetermined...

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Thu May 12, 2011 4:19 pm I don't think 1.11 was missing anything. It's a pretty good movie. And as it stands, IMO, 2.22 was a masterpiece. Also, some people seem to be forgetting that little tidbit in the 2.22 pamphlet. It said Eva2.22 pamphlet wrote: The overall concept of the New Theatrical Edition could compared to that of a railroad. In Prelude, two trains depart from the same starting point, but sooner or later, they come across rail switch points and the familiar scenery starts to change. This is why the introductory part, which tries to start from the same starting point, is called a Prelude.
